text: Minimising the metabolic cost of running, lower limb tissue damage, and muscle fatigability are crucial performance factors in ultra-endurance running events.
Maintaining a sustainable core body temperature is critical to ultra-endurance running performance, supported by strategic heat acclimation and individually calculated hydration.
An even pacing strategy contributes to sustained performance in ultra-endurance running events.
Gastrointestinal issues affect nearly all ultra-endurance running participants and can arise from multiple factors including mechanical stress, reduced blood flow to the gut, and nutritional choices.

Nutritional strategies for ultra-endurance events should be personalised with evidence-based approaches for varying types of carbohydrate, protein, and fat intake in fluid or solid form, and should address flavour fatigue.
Specific long-term and short-term goal setting improves performance in ultra-endurance running.

Fatigue in ultra-endurance running is multi-factorial, encompassing both physical and mental components, with perceived effort and perceived fatigue level having a major impact on ability to sustain pace.
